extraction.article_title: CVE-2026-39987: Marimo RCE exploited in hours after disclosure

ttps.description: Exploit public-facing web application: remote code execution against an exposed Marimo web service.
Command and Scripting Interpreter: likely Python code execution through notebook/app execution features.
OS Credential Dumping: article indicates credential theft shortly after exploitation.

attack_chain: A malicious request triggers the RCE condition in the web application
Arbitrary code executes in the Marimo process context
Attacker retrieves secrets/credentials from memory, config, or filesystem
Credential theft enables further access or pivoting

proposed_kb_patterns.attack_description: A web-exposed notebook or app endpoint accepts attacker-controlled input that is later evaluated as Python code or passed into an execution primitive, allowing arbitrary server-side command execution.
If the application builds shell commands from user input, command separators can break out of the intended argument and execute a second command.
A deserialization or template-evaluation flaw may allow Python object traversal to reach dangerous builtins and invoke system commands without explicitly calling eval.
